Title: Trump's Gaza committee remains an empty shell paralyzed by Israel
Subhead: Created four months ago as part of Donald Trump's peace plan, the entity is supposed to manage the coastal territory in place of Hamas. But Israel is preventing its members from entering, leaving them stranded in their offices in Cairo.

Title: EU's Séjourné warns Europe could splinter without a trade 'power struggle' with China
Norway to join France-led nuclear deterrence program
US military conducts new strikes in southern Iran, drawing retaliation
Subhead: The new attacks come just days after the US military struck several missile sites and mine-laying boats in the same area, in what US officials described at the time as taken in 'self-defense.'

Title: Morocco faces over 40,000 job losses after France bans telemarketing calls
Subhead: A law passed in France bans all prospecting calls without the customer's explicit consent starting August 11. The move endangers tens of thousands of employees in Moroccan call centers, which are heavily dependent on the French market.

Title: In Brittany, luxury tourism is taking over the coastline
Subhead: In Saint-Malo, a court granted approval at the end of March for the construction of a 4- and 5-star hotel complex and a thalassotherapy center on the site of a former municipal campground. This luxury project is one of many springing up across the region.

Title: 'Do you realize that your statements are completely absurd?': Inside the arrest of the Louvre burglars
Subhead: In their new book, journalists Jean-Michel Décugis, Nicolas-Charles Torrent and Le Monde's Jérémie Pham-Lê recount the events of the burglary at the Paris museum on October 19, 2025, and the arrest of the suspects. Here are excerpts from the book.

The post-May '68 Christian philosophy now being embraced by the American far right
Subhead: Trump supporters often blame the cultural upheavals triggered by the May 1968 protests in France for sowing the seeds of 'wokeness.' Yet some of them are now claiming a different French school of thought, which took shape at Stanford University under the influence of René Girard.

Title: French banker lands exceptional and controversial contract to restructure Venezuelan debt
Subhead: Matthieu Pigasse was tapped by Caracas to negotiate the restructuring of Venezuela's massive debt. His competitors suspect Washington played a role.

Title: The network of US pilots helping women seek abortion care
Subhead: Responding to increasingly strict anti-abortion laws in Texas, a non-profit organization offers free flights to neighboring states. The group also assists individuals seeking gender-affirming care.
